title: Polyvagal Theory
blurb: Understanding safety, connection, and the nervous system.
lead: Polyvagal theory offers a way to understand how our nervous system shifts between states of safety, fight-or-flight, and shutdown — and why connection calms us.

blocks.text: Polyvagal theory, proposed by Stephen Porges, describes how the autonomic nervous system moves between states: social engagement (safe and connected), mobilisation (fight-or-flight), and shutdown.
It has become influential in trauma work, offering a compassionate lens on automatic survival responses. It's a theoretical framework; clinical applications are still being researched.

resources.title: Polyvagal theory explained
resources.whyHere: Accessible free explanations of the model.
